commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the standalone root RTT harness and folds packaged Telegram RTT sampling into the qa-lab Telegram live QA lane, emitting aggregate timing in qa-evidence.json and updating Mantis, docs, workflows, and tests.

PR surface: Source +166, Tests -1366, Docs +7, Config -5, Other -2257. Total -3455 across 29 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a QA automation migration. Current main and v2026.6.6 do confirm the shipped pnpm rtt surface that the PR intentionally removes.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer coordination for the intentional shipped command removal, live Telegram/Mantis proof, and paired importer timing; no narrow automated repair remains after the mechanical fixes.

target.title: Fold Telegram RTT sampling into live QA evidence
target.body:
## Summary

- Rebased over openclaw/openclaw#92606 and openclaw/openclaw#92558, keeping native Vitest/Playwright QA evidence and the simplified taxonomy mapping shape intact.
- Fold Telegram package RTT measurement into the normal Telegram live QA lane instead of maintaining a separate `pnpm rtt` command and Telegram-only RTT Docker driver.
- Emit repeated RTT timing through `qa-evidence.json` under `result.timing` for `telegram-mentioned-message-reply`, including avg/p50/p95/max/sample/failure counts, while clearing stale one-off `rttMs` when no repeated RTT checks pass.
- Use first-class package Telegram RTT controls: `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_RTT_SAMPLES`, `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_RTT_TIMEOUT_MS`, `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_RTT_MAX_FAILURES`, and `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_RTT_CHECKS`.
- Keep focused non-RTT scenario runs from forcing the default RTT check, pass injected env through Convex credential leasing, and make RTT-specific timeout authoritative for repeated RTT checks.
- Stop writing the duplicate Telegram observed-message sidecar from the live Telegram lane; mantis keeps a narrow legacy fallback for older artifact directories that still contain it, docs point to the current report/evidence artifacts, and Telegram workflows no longer set the removed capture env.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-catalog.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/test-file-scenario-runner.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts test/scripts/npm-telegram-live.test.ts test/scripts/mantis-build-telegram-evidence.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm oxfmt --check .github/workflows/mantis-telegram-live.yml .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml .github/workflows/npm-telegram-beta-e2e.yml .github/workflows/openclaw-release-checks.yml docs/concepts/mantis.md docs/concepts/qa-e2e-automation.md docs/help/testing.md extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-transport-result.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-transport-rtt.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/cli.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts scripts/e2e/npm-telegram-live-docker.sh scripts/e2e/npm-telegram-live-runner.ts scripts/mantis/build-telegram-evidence.mjs test/scripts/mantis-build-telegram-evidence.test.ts test/scripts/npm-telegram-live.test.ts`
- `python3 scripts/check-composite-action-input-interpolation.py`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --prompt "..."` clean after rebase; legacy `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_WARM/SAMPLE/MAX_FAILURES` aliases intentionally out of scope per requested no-legacy end state and paired openclaw-rtt#15 update.

`pnpm check:workflows` was attempted but blocked before repository checks by the local pre-commit/zizmor environment: `zizmor==1.22.0` requires Python `>=3.10`, while the local pre-commit Python environment can only resolve up to `zizmor 1.16.3`. The direct YAML parse, composite interpolation check, no-conflict-marker check, and workflow guard tests above passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram package RTT measurement now runs through the standard package Telegram live QA lane and writes timing into `qa-evidence.json` instead of a separate RTT command/output shape.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on macOS, using focused unit/schema/script tests plus extension typecheck, docs, formatting, workflow sanity fallbacks, whitespace checks, and autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebasing was completed over #92606 and #92558; ran focused evidence, scenario catalog/native test-file runner, Telegram live runtime, package Telegram live script, and mantis legacy import tests; ran extension typecheck; ran docs list, formatter check, whitespace check, workflow guard fallbacks, and final autoreview.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 6 files / 100 tests across QA evidence/scenario runner/Telegram/package/mantis coverage; `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm docs:list`, focused `oxfmt --check`, `git diff --check`, workflow guard fallbacks, and final autoreview passed.

Observed result after fix: Package Telegram RTT controls flow through package Telegram live, repeated RTT checks are summarized as evidence timing, focused non-RTT runs skip default RTT, and new Telegram live output no longer writes a duplicate observed-message sidecar. Legacy mantis import still tolerates older artifact directories that contain `telegram-qa-observed-messages.json`.

What was not tested: No live Telegram/Convex/Docker lane was executed in this local pass.
